nodes.label: Clients
nodes.description: Users sending requests to the system.

nodes.label: Load Balancer
nodes.description: Distributes incoming requests across the server fleet. Required once you scale out.

nodes.label: App Servers
nodes.description: Stateless application servers. Scaling out means adding more of these; any one can handle any request.

nodes.label: Database
nodes.description: Shared source of truth. Keeping servers stateless pushes state here.

steps.caption: A request arrives and hits the load balancer.
The load balancer spreads requests across the server fleet — scaling out adds more servers here.
Stateless servers read and write shared state in the database.
